root_text.sn35.140:1.2: Yopi hetu, yopi paccayo cakkhussa uppādāya, sopi anicco.
root_text.sn35.140:1.3: Aniccasambhūtaṁ, bhikkhave, cakkhu kuto niccaṁ bhavissati …pe…
root_text.sn35.140:1.5: Yopi hetu, yopi paccayo jivhāya uppādāya sopi anicco.
root_text.sn35.140:1.6: Aniccasambhūtā, bhikkhave, jivhā kuto niccā bhavissati …pe…
root_text.sn35.140:1.8: Yopi, bhikkhave, hetu yopi paccayo manassa uppādāya, sopi anicco.
root_text.sn35.140:1.9: Aniccasambhūto, bhikkhave, mano kuto nicco bhavissati.
root_text.sn35.140:1.10: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako cakkhusmimpi nibbindati …pe… jivhāyapi nibbindati …pe… manasmimpi nibbindati,
root_text.sn35.140:1.11: nibbindaṁ virajjati; virāgā vimuccati; vimuttasmiṁ vimuttamiti ñāṇaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn35.140:1.12: ‘Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānātī”ti.

translation_text.sn35.140:1.2: The cause and reason that gives rise to the eye is also impermanent.
translation_text.sn35.140:1.3: Since the eye is produced by what is impermanent, how could it be permanent?
translation_text.sn35.140:1.8: The cause and reason that gives rise to the mind is also impermanent.
translation_text.sn35.140:1.9: Since the mind is produced by what is impermanent, how could it be permanent?
translation_text.sn35.140:1.10: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned with the eye, ear, nose, tongue, body, and mind.
translation_text.sn35.140:1.11: Being disillusioned, desire fades away. When desire fades away they’re freed. When they’re freed, they know they’re freed.
translation_text.sn35.140:1.12: They understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’”
